1→/*
     2→ * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
     3→ *
     4→ * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
     5→ * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
     6→ * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
     7→ * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
     8→ *
     9→ * Output format: one line per test case
    10→ *   FUNCNAME INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX
    11→ */
    12→#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
    13→#define TEST_COMMON_H
    14→
    15→#include <stdio.h>
    16→#include <math.h>
    17→#include <stdint.h>
    18→#include <string.h>
    19→
    20→static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
    21→{
    22→    uint64_t u;
    23→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    24→    return u;
    25→}
    26→
    27→static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
    28→{
    29→    uint32_t u;
    30→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    31→    return u;
    32→}
    33→
    34→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for double */
    35→#define P_D1(func, input) \
    36→    do { \
    37→        double _in = (input); \
    38→        double _r = func(_in); \
    39→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    40→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    41→    } while (0)
    42→
    43→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for float */
    44→#define P_F1(func, input) \
    45→    do { \
    46→        float _in = (input); \
    47→        float _r = func(_in); \
    48→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x\n", \
    49→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    50→    } while (0)
    51→
    52→/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
    53→#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    54→    do { \
    55→        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
    56→        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
    57→        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    58→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
    59→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    60→    } while (0)
    61→
    62→/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
    63→#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    64→    do { \
    65→        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
    66→        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
    67→        printf("%-12s %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
    68→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    69→    } while (0)
    70→
    71→/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
    72→#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    73→    do { \
    74→        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
    75→        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
    76→        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    77→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
    78→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c), \
    79→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    80→    } while (0)
    81→
    82→/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
    83→#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    84→    do { \
    85→        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
    86→        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
    87→        printf("%-12s %08x %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
    88→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), \
    89→               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    90→    } while (0)
    91→
    92→/* func(input) -> int result */
    93→#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    94→    do { \
    95→        double _in = (input); \
    96→        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
    97→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %lld\n", \
    98→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    99→    } while (0)
   100→
   101→#define P_F1I(func, input) \
   102→    do { \
   103→        float _in = (input); \
   104→        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
   105→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %lld\n", \
   106→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   107→    } while (0)
   108→
   109→/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
   110→#define P_D1B(func, input) \
   111→    do { \
   112→        double _in = (input); \
   113→        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
   114→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
   115→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
   116→    } while (0)
   117→
   118→#define P_F1B(func, input) \
   119→    do { \
   120→        float _in = (input); \
   121→        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
   122→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
   123→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   124→    } while (0)
   125→
   126→/* fpclassify returns an int category */
   127→#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
   128→    do { \
   129→        double _in = (input); \
   130→        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
   131→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
   132→               "fpclassify", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
   133→    } while (0)
   134→
   135→#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
   136→    do { \
   137→        float _in = (input); \
   138→        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
   139→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
   140→               "fpclassifyf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   141→    } while (0)
   142→
   143→/* sincos: two outputs */
   144→#define P_SINCOS(input) \
   145→    do { \
   146→        double _in = (input); \
   147→        double _s, _c; \
   148→        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
   149→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx %016llx\n", \
   150→               "sincos", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), \
   151→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_s), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c)); \
   152→    } while (0)
   153→
   154→#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
   155→    do { \
   156→        float _in = (input); \
   157→        float _s, _c; \
   158→        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
   159→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x %08x\n", \
   160→               "sincosf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), \
   161→               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
   162→    } while (0)
   163→
   164→#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */
   165→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#
     3→# run-all.sh -- Build and compare libm outputs: x86 (glibc) vs RISC-V (picolibc)
     4→#
     5→# Usage:
     6→#   ./run-all.sh [options]
     7→#
     8→# Options:
     9→#   --host-cc CC         Host compiler (default: gcc)
    10→#   --cross-cc CC        Cross compiler (default: riscv64-elf-gcc)
    11→#   --picolibc PATH      Path to picolibc install dir
    12→#   --qemu PATH          QEMU binary (default: qemu-system-riscv64)
    13→#   --qemu-args ARGS     Extra QEMU arguments
    14→#   --ref-only           Only build+run reference (x86)
    15→#   --target-only        Only build+run target (skip x86, use existing ref/)
    16→#   --test NAME          Run only one test (e.g., test-sin)
    17→#
    18→set -e
    19→
    20→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
    21→HOST_CC="${HOST_CC:-gcc}"
    22→CROSS_CC="${CROSS_CC:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
    23→PICOLIBC="${PICOLIBC:-}"
    24→QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-riscv64}"
    25→QEMU_ARGS="${QEMU_ARGS:--M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic}"
    26→REF_ONLY=false
    27→TARGET_ONLY=false
    28→SINGLE_TEST=""
    29→
    30→while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    31→    case "$1" in
    32→        --host-cc)    HOST_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    33→        --cross-cc)   CROSS_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    34→        --picolibc)   PICOLIBC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    35→        --qemu)       QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    36→        --qemu-args)  QEMU_ARGS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    37→        --ref-only)   REF_ONLY=true; shift ;;
    38→        --target-only) TARGET_ONLY=true; shift ;;
    39→        --test)       SINGLE_TEST="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    40→        *)            echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    41→    esac
    42→done
    43→
    44→cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    45→
    46→ALL_TESTS="test-sin test-cos test-sincos test-atan test-exp test-log test-pow \
    47→           test-fabs test-sqrt test-fma test-fmax-fmin test-copysign \
    48→           test-classify test-round"
    49→
    50→if [ -n "$SINGLE_TEST" ]; then
    51→    TESTS="$SINGLE_TEST"
    52→else
    53→    TESTS="$ALL_TESTS"
    54→fi
    55→
    56→mkdir -p ref target ref-out target-out
    57→
    58→# --- Step 1: Build + run x86 reference ---
    59→if [ "$TARGET_ONLY" != true ]; then
    60→    echo "==> Building x86 reference binaries"
    61→    MAKE_ARGS="HOST_CC=$HOST_CC"
    62→    for t in $TESTS; do
    63→        make $MAKE_ARGS "ref/$t"
    64→    done
    65→
    66→    echo "==> Running x86 reference"
    67→    for t in $TESTS; do
    68→        echo "  $t"
    69→        "./ref/$t" > "ref-out/$t.out"
    70→    done
    71→fi
    72→
    73→if [ "$REF_ONLY" = true ]; then
    74→    echo "==> Reference outputs in ref-out/"
    75→    exit 0
    76→fi
    77→
    78→# --- Step 2: Build + run RISC-V target ---
    79→echo "==> Building RISC-V target binaries"
    80→MAKE_ARGS="CROSS_CC=$CROSS_CC"
    81→if [ -n "$PICOLIBC" ]; then
    82→    MAKE_ARGS="$MAKE_ARGS PICOLIBC=$PICOLIBC"
    83→fi
    84→for t in $TESTS; do
    85→    make $MAKE_ARGS "target/$t"
    86→done
    87→
    88→echo "==> Running RISC-V target via QEMU"
    89→for t in $TESTS; do
    90→    echo "  $t"
    91→    $QEMU $QEMU_ARGS -kernel "target/$t" 2>/dev/null > "target-out/$t.out" || true
    92→done
    93→
    94→# --- Step 3: Compare ---
    95→echo ""
    96→echo "==> Comparing outputs"
    97→PASS=0
    98→FAIL=0
    99→ERRORS=""
   100→
   101→for t in $TESTS; do
   102→    REF_FILE="ref-out/$t.out"
   103→    TGT_FILE="target-out/$t.out"
   104→
   105→    if [ ! -f "$REF_FILE" ]; then
   106→        echo "SKIP: $t (no reference output)"
   107→        continue
   108→    fi
   109→    if [ ! -f "$TGT_FILE" ]; then
   110→        echo "SKIP: $t (no target output)"
   111→        continue
   112→    fi
   113→
   114→    if diff -q "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
   115→        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
   116→        echo "  PASS: $t"
   117→    else
   118→        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
   119→        ERRORS="$ERRORS $t"
   120→        echo "  FAIL: $t"
   121→        diff -u "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" | head -20
   122→        echo "  ..."
   123→    fi
   124→done
   125→
   126→echo ""
   127→echo "==============================="
   128→echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
   129→if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
   130→    echo "Failed:$ERRORS"
   131→    exit 1
   132→fi
   133→echo "==============================="
   134→exit 0
   135→

1→# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
     2→#
     3→# Two-target approach:
     4→#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
     5→#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc via QEMU) -> target output
     6→#   3. diff the outputs
     7→#
     8→# Usage:
     9→#   make ref-bins       # Build x86 reference binaries
    10→#   make target-bins    # Build RISC-V target binaries (needs CROSS_CC + PICOLIBC)
    11→#   make clean
    12→
    13→HOST_CC   ?= gcc
    14→CROSS_CC  ?= riscv64-elf-gcc

toolUseResult.prompt:
Review this new test suite for code quality issues. All files are in test/libm-accuracy/.

Key concerns to check:

1. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: test-sin.c, test-cos.c, test-sincos.c all have nearly identical din[] and fin[] arrays. Is this worth deduplicating?

2. **test-common.h macro quality**:
   - P_D1B uses `!!` to normalize bool — is this correct for all classification functions?
   - isinf/isnan are macros in C99, not functions — does `#func` stringification work correctly with P_D1B/P_F1B when func is a macro?
   - P_FPCL_D/P_FPCL_F hardcode the string "fpclassify"/"fpclassifyf" — inconsistent with other macros that use #func
   - Do any macros have naming collisions with variables (_in, _r, _a, _b, _c)?

3. **test-classify.c**: Uses P_D1B with `isinf`, `isnan`, `finite` — but isinf/isnan are C99 macros (type-generic). The `#func` in P_D1B will stringify the *macro expansion*, not the name. This could produce garbled output or compile errors.

4. **test-sincos.c**: Has both `#define _GNU_SOURCE` and `extern` declarations for sincos/sincosf. The extern decls may conflict with the declarations brought in by _GNU_SOURCE + math.h.

5. **run-all.sh**: Uses `set -e` but the QEMU run has `|| true` — is the error handling consistent?

6. **Makefile**: The `mkdir -p` in every recipe is redundant after the first build.

Be concrete with file paths and specific issues.

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#
     3→# run-all.sh -- Build and compare libm outputs: x86 (glibc) vs RISC-V (picolibc)
     4→#
     5→# Usage:
     6→#   ./run-all.sh [options]
     7→#
     8→# Options:
     9→#   --host-cc CC         Host compiler (default: gcc)
    10→#   --cross-cc CC        Cross compiler (default: riscv64-elf-gcc)
    11→#   --picolibc PATH      Path to picolibc install dir
    12→#   --qemu PATH          QEMU binary (default: qemu-system-riscv64)
    13→#   --qemu-args ARGS     Extra QEMU arguments
    14→#   --ref-only           Only build+run reference (x86)
    15→#   --target-only        Only build+run target (skip x86, use existing ref/)
    16→#   --test NAME          Run only one test (e.g., test-sin)
    17→#
    18→set -e
    19→
    20→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
    21→HOST_CC="${HOST_CC:-gcc}"
    22→CROSS_CC="${CROSS_CC:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
    23→PICOLIBC="${PICOLIBC:-}"
    24→QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-riscv64}"
    25→QEMU_ARGS="${QEMU_ARGS:--M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic}"
    26→REF_ONLY=false
    27→TARGET_ONLY=false
    28→SINGLE_TEST=""
    29→
    30→while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    31→    case "$1" in
    32→        --host-cc)    HOST_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    33→        --cross-cc)   CROSS_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    34→        --picolibc)   PICOLIBC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    35→        --qemu)       QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    36→        --qemu-args)  QEMU_ARGS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    37→        --ref-only)   REF_ONLY=true; shift ;;
    38→        --target-only) TARGET_ONLY=true; shift ;;
    39→        --test)       SINGLE_TEST="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    40→        *)            echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    41→    esac
    42→done
    43→
    44→cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    45→
    46→ALL_TESTS="test-sin test-cos test-sincos test-atan test-exp test-log test-pow \
    47→           test-fabs test-sqrt test-fma test-fmax-fmin test-copysign \
    48→           test-classify test-round"
    49→
    50→if [ -n "$SINGLE_TEST" ]; then
    51→    TESTS="$SINGLE_TEST"
    52→else
    53→    TESTS="$ALL_TESTS"
    54→fi
    55→
    56→mkdir -p ref target ref-out target-out
    57→
    58→# --- Step 1: Build + run x86 reference ---
    59→if [ "$TARGET_ONLY" != true ]; then
    60→    echo "==> Building x86 reference binaries"
    61→    MAKE_ARGS="HOST_CC=$HOST_CC"
    62→    for t in $TESTS; do
    63→        make $MAKE_ARGS "ref/$t"
    64→    done
    65→
    66→    echo "==> Running x86 reference"
    67→    for t in $TESTS; do
    68→        echo "  $t"
    69→        "./ref/$t" > "ref-out/$t.out"
    70→    done
    71→fi
    72→
    73→if [ "$REF_ONLY" = true ]; then
    74→    echo "==> Reference outputs in ref-out/"
    75→    exit 0
    76→fi
    77→
    78→# --- Step 2: Build + run RISC-V target ---
    79→echo "==> Building RISC-V target binaries"
    80→MAKE_ARGS="CROSS_CC=$CROSS_CC"
    81→if [ -n "$PICOLIBC" ]; then
    82→    MAKE_ARGS="$MAKE_ARGS PICOLIBC=$PICOLIBC"
    83→fi
    84→for t in $TESTS; do
    85→    make $MAKE_ARGS "target/$t"
    86→done
    87→
    88→echo "==> Running RISC-V target via QEMU"
    89→for t in $TESTS; do
    90→    echo "  $t"
    91→    $QEMU $QEMU_ARGS -kernel "target/$t" 2>/dev/null > "target-out/$t.out" || true
    92→done
    93→
    94→# --- Step 3: Compare ---
    95→echo ""
    96→echo "==> Comparing outputs"
    97→PASS=0
    98→FAIL=0
    99→ERRORS=""
   100→
   101→for t in $TESTS; do
   102→    REF_FILE="ref-out/$t.out"
   103→    TGT_FILE="target-out/$t.out"
   104→
   105→    if [ ! -f "$REF_FILE" ]; then
   106→        echo "SKIP: $t (no reference output)"
   107→        continue
   108→    fi
   109→    if [ ! -f "$TGT_FILE" ]; then
   110→        echo "SKIP: $t (no target output)"
   111→        continue
   112→    fi
   113→
   114→    if diff -q "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
   115→        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
   116→        echo "  PASS: $t"
   117→    else
   118→        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
   119→        ERRORS="$ERRORS $t"
   120→        echo "  FAIL: $t"
   121→        diff -u "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" | head -20
   122→        echo "  ..."
   123→    fi
   124→done
   125→
   126→echo ""
   127→echo "==============================="
   128→echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
   129→if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
   130→    echo "Failed:$ERRORS"
   131→    exit 1
   132→fi
   133→echo "==============================="
   134→exit 0
   135→

1→# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
     2→#
     3→# Two-target approach:
     4→#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
     5→#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc via QEMU) -> target output
     6→#   3. diff the outputs
     7→#
     8→# Usage:
     9→#   make ref-bins       # Build x86 reference binaries
    10→#   make target-bins    # Build RISC-V target binaries (needs CROSS_CC + PICOLIBC)
    11→#   make clean
    12→
    13→HOST_CC   ?= gcc
    14→CROSS_CC  ?= riscv64-elf-gcc
    15→CFLAGS    ?= -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter
    16→
    17→# picolibc install path (must contain lib/picolibc.specs or similar)
    18→ifdef PICOLIBC
    19→  SPECS = --specs=$(PICOLIBC)/picolibc.specs
    20→else
    21→  SPECS = --specs=picolibc.specs
    22→endif
    23→
    24→TESTS = test-sin test-cos test-sincos test-atan test-exp test-log test-pow \
    25→        test-fabs test-sqrt test-fma test-fmax-fmin test-copysign \
    26→        test-classify test-round
    27→
    28→REF_BINS    = $(addprefix ref/,$(TESTS))
    29→TARGET_BINS = $(addprefix target/,$(TESTS))
    30→
    31→.PHONY: ref-bins target-bins clean
    32→
    33→ref-bins: $(REF_BINS)
    34→
    35→target-bins: $(TARGET_BINS)
    36→
    37→ref/%: %.c test-common.h
    38→	@mkdir -p ref
    39→	$(HOST_CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm
    40→
    41→target/%: %.c test-common.h
    42→	@mkdir -p target
    43→	$(CROSS_CC) $(SPECS) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm
    44→
    45→clean:
    46→	rm -rf ref target ref-out target-out
    47→

1→/*
     2→ * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
     3→ *
     4→ * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
     5→ * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
     6→ * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
     7→ * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
     8→ *
     9→ * Output format: one line per test case
    10→ *   FUNCNAME INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX
    11→ */
    12→#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
    13→#define TEST_COMMON_H
    14→
    15→#include <stdio.h>
    16→#include <math.h>
    17→#include <stdint.h>
    18→#include <string.h>
    19→
    20→static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
    21→{
    22→    uint64_t u;
    23→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    24→    return u;
    25→}
    26→
    27→static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
    28→{
    29→    uint32_t u;
    30→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    31→    return u;
    32→}
    33→
    34→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for double */
    35→#define P_D1(func, input) \
    36→    do { \
    37→        double _in = (input); \
    38→        double _r = func(_in); \
    39→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    40→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    41→    } while (0)
    42→
    43→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for float */
    44→#define P_F1(func, input) \
    45→    do { \
    46→        float _in = (input); \
    47→        float _r = func(_in); \
    48→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x\n", \
    49→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    50→    } while (0)
    51→
    52→/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
    53→#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    54→    do { \
    55→        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
    56→        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
    57→        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    58→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
    59→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    60→    } while (0)
    61→
    62→/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
    63→#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    64→    do { \
    65→        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
    66→        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
    67→        printf("%-12s %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
    68→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    69→    } while (0)
    70→
    71→/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
    72→#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    73→    do { \
    74→        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
    75→        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
    76→        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    77→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
    78→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c), \
    79→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    80→    } while (0)
    81→
    82→/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
    83→#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    84→    do { \
    85→        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
    86→        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
    87→        printf("%-12s %08x %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
    88→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), \
    89→               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    90→    } while (0)
    91→
    92→/* func(input) -> int result */
    93→#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    94→    do { \
    95→        double _in = (input); \
    96→        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
    97→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %lld\n", \
    98→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    99→    } while (0)
   100→
   101→#define P_F1I(func, input) \
   102→    do { \
   103→        float _in = (input); \
   104→        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
   105→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %lld\n", \
   106→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   107→    } while (0)
   108→
   109→/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
   110→#define P_D1B(func, input) \
   111→    do { \
   112→        double _in = (input); \
   113→        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
   114→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
   115→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
   116→    } while (0)
   117→
   118→#define P_F1B(func, input) \
   119→    do { \
   120→        float _in = (input); \
   121→        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
   122→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
   123→               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   124→    } while (0)
   125→
   126→/* fpclassify returns an int category */
   127→#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
   128→    do { \
   129→        double _in = (input); \
   130→        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
   131→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
   132→               "fpclassify", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
   133→    } while (0)
   134→
   135→#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
   136→    do { \
   137→        float _in = (input); \
   138→        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
   139→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
   140→               "fpclassifyf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
   141→    } while (0)
   142→
   143→/* sincos: two outputs */
   144→#define P_SINCOS(input) \
   145→    do { \
   146→        double _in = (input); \
   147→        double _s, _c; \
   148→        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
   149→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx %016llx\n", \
   150→               "sincos", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), \
   151→               (unsigned long long)d2u(_s), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c)); \
   152→    } while (0)
   153→
   154→#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
   155→    do { \
   156→        float _in = (input); \
   157→        float _s, _c; \
   158→        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
   159→        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x %08x\n", \
   160→               "sincosf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), \
   161→               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
   162→    } while (0)
   163→
   164→#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */
   165→

Review this new test suite in test/libm-accuracy/ for efficiency issues.

Key files: test-common.h (macros), 14 test-*.c files, Makefile, run-all.sh

Check for:

2. **Makefile**: Are there missing dependencies or unnecessary rebuilds?

3. **printf in macros**: Each test case calls printf individually. For embedded targets with slow semihosting I/O, is this a concern? Could buffering help?

4. **QEMU invocations in run-all.sh**: Each test spawns a separate QEMU instance. Is there a way to batch them?

5. **Unnecessary work**: Are there any redundant computations in the test files themselves?

6. **run-all.sh comparison**: Uses `diff -q` then `diff -u` on failure — the file is read twice on failure. Minor but worth noting.

Be concrete — identify specific lines and suggest fixes only for real issues.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
 *
 * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
 * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
 * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
 * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
 *
 * Output format: one line per test case
 *   FUNCNAME INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX
 */
#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
#define TEST_COMMON_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <string.h>

static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
{
    uint64_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
{
    uint32_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for double */
#define P_D1(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _r = func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for float */
#define P_F1(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _r = func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("%-12s %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("%-12s %08x %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), \
               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* func(input) -> int result */
#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
#define P_D1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* fpclassify returns an int category */
#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
               "fpclassify", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
               "fpclassifyf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* sincos: two outputs */
#define P_SINCOS(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _s, _c; \
        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx %016llx\n", \
               "sincos", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_s), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _s, _c; \
        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x %08x\n", \
               "sincosf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), \
               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */

content.text: Now fix `run-all.sh` — derive test list from filesystem and use single `make` call.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# run-all.sh -- Build and compare libm outputs: x86 (glibc) vs RISC-V (picolibc)
#
# Usage:
#   ./run-all.sh [options]
#
# Options:
#   --host-cc CC         Host compiler (default: gcc)
#   --cross-cc CC        Cross compiler (default: riscv64-elf-gcc)
#   --picolibc PATH      Path to picolibc install dir
#   --qemu PATH          QEMU binary (default: qemu-system-riscv64)
#   --qemu-args ARGS     Extra QEMU arguments
#   --ref-only           Only build+run reference (x86)
#   --target-only        Only build+run target (skip x86, use existing ref/)
#   --test NAME          Run only one test (e.g., test-sin)
#
set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
HOST_CC="${HOST_CC:-gcc}"
CROSS_CC="${CROSS_CC:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
PICOLIBC="${PICOLIBC:-}"
QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-riscv64}"
QEMU_ARGS="${QEMU_ARGS:--M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic}"
REF_ONLY=false
TARGET_ONLY=false
SINGLE_TEST=""

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --host-cc)    HOST_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cross-cc)   CROSS_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --picolibc)   PICOLIBC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)       QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-args)  QEMU_ARGS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --ref-only)   REF_ONLY=true; shift ;;
        --target-only) TARGET_ONLY=true; shift ;;
        --test)       SINGLE_TEST="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *)            echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

# Derive test list from test-*.c files
if [ -n "$SINGLE_TEST" ]; then
    TESTS="$SINGLE_TEST"
else
    TESTS=$(ls test-*.c 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\.c$//' | grep -v common | sort)
fi

if [ -z "$TESTS" ]; then
    echo "Error: no test-*.c files found"
    exit 1
fi

# Build make args
MAKE_ARGS=""
if [ -n "$PICOLIBC" ]; then
    MAKE_ARGS="PICOLIBC=$PICOLIBC"
fi

# --- Step 1: Build + run x86 reference ---
if [ "$TARGET_ONLY" != true ]; then
    echo "==> Building x86 reference binaries"
    REF_TARGETS=$(echo "$TESTS" | sed 's|^|ref/|')
    make HOST_CC="$HOST_CC" $REF_TARGETS

    echo "==> Running x86 reference"
    mkdir -p ref-out
    for t in $TESTS; do
        echo "  $t"
        "./ref/$t" > "ref-out/$t.out"
    done
fi

if [ "$REF_ONLY" = true ]; then
    echo "==> Reference outputs in ref-out/"
    exit 0
fi

# --- Step 2: Build + run RISC-V target ---
echo "==> Building RISC-V target binaries"
TGT_TARGETS=$(echo "$TESTS" | sed 's|^|target/|')
make CROSS_CC="$CROSS_CC" $MAKE_ARGS $TGT_TARGETS

echo "==> Running RISC-V target via QEMU"
mkdir -p target-out
for t in $TESTS; do
    echo "  $t"
    $QEMU $QEMU_ARGS -kernel "target/$t" 2>/dev/null > "target-out/$t.out" || true
done

# --- Step 3: Compare ---
echo ""
echo "==> Comparing outputs"
PASS=0
FAIL=0
ERRORS=""

for t in $TESTS; do
    REF_FILE="ref-out/$t.out"
    TGT_FILE="target-out/$t.out"

    if [ ! -f "$REF_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no reference output)"
        continue
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$TGT_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no target output)"
        continue
    fi

    if diff -q "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
        echo "  PASS: $t"
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
        ERRORS="$ERRORS $t"
        echo "  FAIL: $t"
        diff -u "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" | head -20
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "==============================="
echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed:$ERRORS"
    exit 1
fi
echo "==============================="
exit 0

    exit 0
fi

# --- Step 2: Build + run RISC-V target ---
echo "==> Building RISC-V target binaries"
TGT_TARGETS=$(echo "$TESTS" | sed 's|^|target/|')
make CROSS_CC="$CROSS_CC" $MAKE_ARGS $TGT_TARGETS

echo "==> Running RISC-V target via QEMU"
mkdir -p target-out
for t in $TESTS; do
    echo "  $t"
    $QEMU $QEMU_ARGS -kernel "target/$t" 2>/dev/null > "target-out/$t.out" || true
done

# --- Step 3: Compare ---
echo ""
echo "==> Comparing outputs"
PASS=0
FAIL=0
ERRORS=""

for t in $TESTS; do
    REF_FILE="ref-out/$t.out"
    TGT_FILE="target-out/$t.out"

    if [ ! -f "$REF_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no reference output)"
        continue
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$TGT_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no target output)"
        continue
    fi

    if diff -q "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
        echo "  PASS: $t"
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
        ERRORS="$ERRORS $t"
        echo "  FAIL: $t"
        diff -u "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" | head -20
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "==============================="
echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed:$ERRORS"
    exit 1
fi
echo "==============================="
exit 0

+    TESTS=$(ls test-*.c 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\.c$//' | grep -v common | sort)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# run-all.sh -- Build and compare libm outputs: x86 (glibc) vs RISC-V (picolibc)
#
# Usage:
#   ./run-all.sh [options]
#
# Options:
#   --host-cc CC         Host compiler (default: gcc)
#   --cross-cc CC        Cross compiler (default: riscv64-elf-gcc)
#   --picolibc PATH      Path to picolibc install dir
#   --qemu PATH          QEMU binary (default: qemu-system-riscv64)
#   --qemu-args ARGS     Extra QEMU arguments
#   --ref-only           Only build+run reference (x86)
#   --target-only        Only build+run target (skip x86, use existing ref/)
#   --test NAME          Run only one test (e.g., test-sin)
#
set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
HOST_CC="${HOST_CC:-gcc}"
CROSS_CC="${CROSS_CC:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
PICOLIBC="${PICOLIBC:-}"
QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-riscv64}"
QEMU_ARGS="${QEMU_ARGS:--M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic}"
REF_ONLY=false
TARGET_ONLY=false
SINGLE_TEST=""

while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
    case "$1" in
        --host-cc)    HOST_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cross-cc)   CROSS_CC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --picolibc)   PICOLIBC="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)       QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-args)  QEMU_ARGS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --ref-only)   REF_ONLY=true; shift ;;
        --target-only) TARGET_ONLY=true; shift ;;
        --test)       SINGLE_TEST="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        *)            echo "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
    esac
done

cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

ALL_TESTS="test-sin test-cos test-sincos test-atan test-exp test-log test-pow \
           test-fabs test-sqrt test-fma test-fmax-fmin test-copysign \
           test-classify test-round"

if [ -n "$SINGLE_TEST" ]; then
    TESTS="$SINGLE_TEST"
else
    TESTS="$ALL_TESTS"
fi

mkdir -p ref target ref-out target-out

# --- Step 1: Build + run x86 reference ---
if [ "$TARGET_ONLY" != true ]; then
    echo "==> Building x86 reference binaries"
    MAKE_ARGS="HOST_CC=$HOST_CC"
    for t in $TESTS; do
        make $MAKE_ARGS "ref/$t"
    done

    echo "==> Running x86 reference"
    for t in $TESTS; do
        echo "  $t"
        "./ref/$t" > "ref-out/$t.out"
    done
fi

if [ "$REF_ONLY" = true ]; then
    echo "==> Reference outputs in ref-out/"
    exit 0
fi

# --- Step 2: Build + run RISC-V target ---
echo "==> Building RISC-V target binaries"
MAKE_ARGS="CROSS_CC=$CROSS_CC"
if [ -n "$PICOLIBC" ]; then
    MAKE_ARGS="$MAKE_ARGS PICOLIBC=$PICOLIBC"
fi
for t in $TESTS; do
    make $MAKE_ARGS "target/$t"
done

echo "==> Running RISC-V target via QEMU"
for t in $TESTS; do
    echo "  $t"
    $QEMU $QEMU_ARGS -kernel "target/$t" 2>/dev/null > "target-out/$t.out" || true
done

# --- Step 3: Compare ---
echo ""
echo "==> Comparing outputs"
PASS=0
FAIL=0
ERRORS=""

for t in $TESTS; do
    REF_FILE="ref-out/$t.out"
    TGT_FILE="target-out/$t.out"

    if [ ! -f "$REF_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no reference output)"
        continue
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$TGT_FILE" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: $t (no target output)"
        continue
    fi

    if diff -q "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
        echo "  PASS: $t"
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
        ERRORS="$ERRORS $t"
        echo "  FAIL: $t"
        diff -u "$REF_FILE" "$TGT_FILE" | head -20
        echo "  ..."
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "==============================="
echo "Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed:$ERRORS"
    exit 1
fi
echo "==============================="
exit 0

# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
#
# Two-target approach:
#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc via QEMU) -> target output
#   3. diff the outputs
#
# Usage:
#   make ref-bins       # Build x86 reference binaries
#   make target-bins    # Build RISC-V target binaries (needs CROSS_CC + PICOLIBC)
#   make clean

HOST_CC   ?= gcc
CROSS_CC  ?= riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS    ?= -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter

ifdef PICOLIBC
  SPECS = --specs=$(PICOLIBC)/picolibc.specs
else
  SPECS = --specs=picolibc.specs
endif

TESTS       = $(patsubst %.c,%,$(filter-out test-common.h,$(wildcard test-*.c)))
HEADERS     = test-common.h test-trig-data.h
REF_BINS    = $(addprefix ref/,$(TESTS))
TARGET_BINS = $(addprefix target/,$(TESTS))

.PHONY: ref-bins target-bins clean

ref-bins: $(REF_BINS)

target-bins: $(TARGET_BINS)

ref/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p ref
	$(HOST_CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

target/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p target
	$(CROSS_CC) $(SPECS) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

clean:
	rm -rf ref target ref-out target-out

content.text:
Here's a summary of what was fixed:

**Code reuse fixes:**
- Extracted duplicate `din[]`/`fin[]` trig arrays from test-sin.c, test-cos.c, test-sincos.c into shared `test-trig-data.h`
- Added `RUN_D1_SUITE`/`RUN_F1_SUITE`/`RUN_SINCOS_SUITE`/`RUN_SINCOSF_SUITE` macros to test-common.h, eliminating the identical loop+special-value boilerplate from 6 test files
- test-sincos.c now tests the same 4 near-pi/near-pi/2 edge cases that sin/cos test (was a copy-paste omission)
- test-exp.c and test-log.c folded their special cases into the array + RUN macro (removed duplicate manual calls)

**Quality fixes:**
- Test list no longer duplicated between Makefile and run-all.sh — both now derive it from `test-*.c` files on disk
- Makefile `HEADERS` dependency includes `test-trig-data.h` so changes trigger rebuild

**Efficiency fixes:**
- `run-all.sh` now calls `make` once per phase (single `make ref/test-*` call) instead of looping `make ref/$t` 14 times

**Not fixed (false positives):**
- `isinf`/`isnan` macro stringification: verified that `#func` correctly produces the literal name "isinf" before macro expansion — works on both glibc and picolibc
- `fpclassify` constants: verified FP_NAN/FP_INFINITE/FP_ZERO/FP_SUBNORMAL/FP_NORMAL have identical values in glibc and picolibc
- `mkdir -p` in Makefile recipes: harmless, avoids dependency on directory order

The command produced no output after 5 seconds. This is consistent with the binary not being compiled with `-mvh` (Andes virtual hosting / semihosting), which would cause QEMU to hang with no I/O.

To get output from the binary, it needs to be compiled with the `-mvh` flag so that semihosting I/O is enabled. Without it, any calls to `printf`, `puts`, etc. will not be routed through the QEMU semihosting interface and the program will hang or produce nothing.

If you can recompile `/tmp/test-fabs` with `-mvh` added to the compiler flags, re-running it should produce output.

   101→if [ -z "$options" ]; then
   102→    options="$(echo "$elf" | sed 's/.*rv[36][24]\([a-z]*\)_.*$/\1/' | sed 's/\(.\)/\1 /g')"
   103→fi

   128→

#!/bin/sh
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
#
# Copyright © 2019 Keith Packard
#
# Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
# modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions
# are met:
#
# 1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright
#    notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
#
# 2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above
#    copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following
#    disclaimer in the documentation and/or other materials provided
#    with the distribution.
#
# 3. Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its
#    contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived
#    from this software without specific prior written permission.
#
# THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS
# "AS IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT
# LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS
# FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
# COPYRIGHT HOLDER OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT,
# INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES
# (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR
# SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
# HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT,
# STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE)
# ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED
# OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
#

qemu="qemu-system-riscv32"

# select the program
elf="$1"
shift

done=0
while [ $# -gt 0 -a $done -eq 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --stdin)
      if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then
        echo "Error: --stdin requires a quoted string"
        exit 1
      fi
      stdin="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --args)
      if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then
        echo "Error: --args requires a quoted string"
        exit 1
      fi
      args="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    *)
      done=1
      ;;
  esac
done

archstring=""
# If readelf is installed, we can use the .riscv.attributes section
# in the file to determine required extensions that we need in QEMU.
# This will give us a string such as "rv64i2p0_m2p0_a2p0_f2p0_d2p0_c2p0"
if command -v readelf >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; then
    # LLVM-based toolchains readelf produces different output from GNU, so
    # check --version output first.
    if readelf --version | grep LLVM >/dev/null; then
        archstring=$(readelf --arch-specific "$elf" | grep "Value: rv" | cut -d: -f2)
    else
        archstring=$(readelf --arch-specific "$elf" | grep Tag_RISCV_arch | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d '"')
    fi
fi
if [ -z "$archstring" ]; then
    echo "Could not determine architecture for $elf, is readelf installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi
if [ "${archstring#*rv64}" != "$archstring" ]; then
    qemu="qemu-system-riscv64"
    cpu="rv64"
elif [ "${archstring#*rv32}" != "$archstring" ]; then
    qemu="qemu-system-riscv32"
    cpu="rv32"
else
    echo "Unknown architecture for $elf: $archstring" >&2
    exit 1
fi
options="$(echo "$archstring" | sed 's/rv[36][24]//' | sed 's/_/ /g' | sed 's/[0-9]p[0-9]\>//g')"

qemu_version="$("$qemu" --version | grep version | sed 's/.*version \([0-9][0-9.]*\).*/\1/')"

cpu="$cpu,mmu=false,pmp=false"

if [ -z "$options" ]; then
    options="$(echo "$elf" | sed 's/.*rv[36][24]\([a-z]*\)_.*$/\1/' | sed 's/\(.\)/\1 /g')"
fi

for o in $options; do
    # Old versions of qemu don't have new extension subsets
    case "$qemu_version" in
        8*|7*)
            case "$o" in
                zmmul)
                    o=m
                    ;;
                zaamo|zalrsc|zawrs)
                    o=a
                    ;;
            esac
            ;;
    esac
    case "$o" in
        zvl*)
            # qemu doesn't list zvl extensions
            ;;
        *)
            cpu="$cpu,$o=true"
            ;;
    esac
done

# qemu enables some options by defualt; we need to explicitly
# disable them when not selected
enabled_options="i h e g m a f d c s u v zifencei zihintpause"

# Add default enabled options for newer qemu versions
case "$qemu_version" in
    8.[01]*|7*)
        ;;
    *)
        enabled_options="$enabled_options zawrs zfa zihintntl"
        ;;
esac

for enabled in $enabled_options; do
    if echo "$options" | grep -q -w $enabled; then
        :
    else
        cpu="$cpu,$enabled=false"
    fi
done

# Map lower case options to upper case for qemu version 8.1 and earlier
case "$qemu_version" in
    8.[01]*|7*)
        cpu="$(echo "$cpu" | sed 's/\<z/Z/g')"
        ;;
esac

# Set the target machine
machine=virt,accel=tcg

# Map stdio to a multiplexed character device so we can use it
# for the monitor and semihosting output

chardev=stdio,mux=on,id=stdio0

# Point the semihosting driver at our new chardev

semi=enable=on,chardev=stdio0,arg="program-name $args"

# Disable monitor

mon=none

# Disable serial

serial=none

echo $stdin | \
  "$qemu" \
    -chardev $chardev \
    -semihosting-config "$semi" \
    -monitor "$mon" \
    -serial "$serial" \
    -machine "$machine" \
    -cpu "$cpu" \
    -kernel "$elf" \
    -nographic \
    -bios none \
    -m 2G

content.text:
The issue is that both our standalone binary and picolibc's meson tests have `xandes5p0` in their RISC-V arch attributes (from the Andes toolchain). The `run-riscv` script parses this and passes `-cpu ...,xandes=true` to QEMU.

The meson tests work because the Andes QEMU at `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/` is on PATH and presumably supports the `xandes` CPU property. But when I ran `run-riscv` directly, I suspect the **system** QEMU (`/usr/bin/qemu-system-riscv64`) was being picked up instead — and upstream QEMU doesn't know about `xandes`.

The fix is straightforward: ensure the Andes QEMU is first in `$PATH` when running. Let me verify and run the full suite.

content.content: Exit code 1

content.text:
The binary exited with code 1 and produced no output. This typically means the test failed (non-zero exit) but wrote nothing to stdout or stderr, or that the binary was not compiled with `-mvh` (Andes virtual hosting / semihosting), which is required for I/O to reach the console under QEMU.

The compile command shown uses `--oslib=semihost` but does not include `-mvh`. Without `-mvh`, QEMU semihosting calls are not emitted and the program will hang or produce no output. Recompile adding `-mvh` to the flags:

```
/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc \
  --specs=/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/build-riscv64-test/picolibc.specs \
  -O2 --oslib=semihost -mvh \
  -o /tmp/test-fabs-sh \
  /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/test/libm-accuracy/test-fabs.c -lm
```

Then re-run the binary.

message.content: how do you build /tmp/test-fabs-mvh?

content.text:
The command I used was:

```
/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc \
  --specs=/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/build-riscv64-test/picolibc.specs \
  -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter --oslib=semihost -mvh \
  -o /tmp/test-fabs-mvh \
  test-fabs.c -lm
```

But we found that `--oslib=semihost` + `-mvh` conflict — picolibc's semihost library and Andes virtual hosting provide competing `write`/`_exit` implementations. The working combination was `--oslib=gloss_vh` + `-mvh` (using the Andes `libgloss_vh.a` instead of picolibc's `libsemihost.a`).

message.content: why do we need --specs=/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/build-riscv64-test/picolibc.specs

content.text:
To use picolibc's headers and libraries (including its libm) instead of the toolchain's default newlib. That's the whole point — we're testing **picolibc's** math functions.

Without `--specs`, the compiler uses newlib's libm, and we'd just be comparing newlib-on-RISC-V vs glibc-on-x86, which isn't what we want.

message.content: the spec is too complicate. could we just specify the rebuilt libm to link before linker finds the default one from toolchain. ! understand?

content.text:
Yes — just put picolibc's `libm.a` first on the link line so the linker resolves math symbols from it before falling back to the toolchain's default. No specs file needed.

```
riscv64-elf-gcc -mvh -O2 -o test-fabs test-fabs.c \
  /path/to/picolibc/build/libm/libm.a -lm
```

The linker processes left-to-right: it finds `sin`, `cos`, etc. in picolibc's `libm.a` first, and never looks at the toolchain's `-lm`.

Let me find the right path and verify it works.

It runs! The output is garbled because picolibc's `printf` is being mixed with newlib's I/O layer. The `"..., 50) = 50` is the semihosting `write()` return value leaking through. This is because we're linking picolibc's `libc.a` (which has its own printf/stdio) but newlib's `libgloss_vh` (which has a different `write` signature).

The fix: we also need picolibc's `printf` to match newlib's `write(fd, buf, count)` convention. Let me check what our recent commit `50525d7c5` did — it switched picolibc to POSIX `write()`.

input.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
#
# Two-target approach:
#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc libm) -> target output
#   3. diff the outputs
#
# For RISC-V: links picolibc's libc.a (which contains libm) before the
# toolchain's default libs, so picolibc's math functions take priority.
# Uses -mvh for Andes virtual hosting (QEMU semihosting I/O).
#
# Usage:
#   make ref-bins                       # Build x86 reference binaries
#   make target-bins PICOLIBC_LIBC=...  # Build RISC-V target binaries
#   make clean

HOST_CC    ?= gcc
CROSS_CC   ?= riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS     ?= -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter

# Path to picolibc's libc.a (contains libm)
PICOLIBC_LIBC ?=

TESTS       = $(patsubst %.c,%,$(filter-out test-common.h,$(wildcard test-*.c)))
HEADERS     = test-common.h test-trig-data.h
REF_BINS    = $(addprefix ref/,$(TESTS))
TARGET_BINS = $(addprefix target/,$(TESTS))

.PHONY: ref-bins target-bins clean

ref-bins: $(REF_BINS)

target-bins: $(TARGET_BINS)

ref/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p ref
	$(HOST_CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

target/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p target
ifndef PICOLIBC_LIBC
	$(error PICOLIBC_LIBC not set. Point it at picolibc's libc.a)
endif
	$(CROSS_CC) -mvh $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(PICOLIBC_LIBC) -lm

clean:
	rm -rf ref target ref-out target-out

# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
#
# Two-target approach:
#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc libm) -> target output
#   3. diff the outputs
#
# For RISC-V: links picolibc's libc.a (which contains libm) before the
# toolchain's default libs, so picolibc's math functions take priority.
# Uses -mvh for Andes virtual hosting (QEMU semihosting I/O).
#
# Usage:
#   make ref-bins                       # Build x86 reference binaries
#   make target-bins PICOLIBC_LIBC=...  # Build RISC-V target binaries
#   make clean

HOST_CC    ?= gcc
CROSS_CC   ?= riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS     ?= -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter

# Path to picolibc's libc.a (contains libm)
PICOLIBC_LIBC ?=

TESTS       = $(patsubst %.c,%,$(filter-out test-common.h,$(wildcard test-*.c)))
HEADERS     = test-common.h test-trig-data.h
REF_BINS    = $(addprefix ref/,$(TESTS))
TARGET_BINS = $(addprefix target/,$(TESTS))

.PHONY: ref-bins target-bins clean

ref-bins: $(REF_BINS)

target-bins: $(TARGET_BINS)

ref/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p ref
	$(HOST_CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

target/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p target
ifndef PICOLIBC_LIBC
	$(error PICOLIBC_LIBC not set. Point it at picolibc's libc.a)
endif
	$(CROSS_CC) -mvh $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(PICOLIBC_LIBC) -lm

clean:
	rm -rf ref target ref-out target-out

lines: #   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
-#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc via QEMU) -> target output
+#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc libm) -> target output
+# For RISC-V: links picolibc's libc.a (which contains libm) before the
+# toolchain's default libs, so picolibc's math functions take priority.
+# Uses -mvh for Andes virtual hosting (QEMU semihosting I/O).
-#   make ref-bins       # Build x86 reference binaries
-#   make target-bins    # Build RISC-V target binaries (needs CROSS_CC + PICOLIBC)
+#   make ref-bins                       # Build x86 reference binaries
+#   make target-bins PICOLIBC_LIBC=...  # Build RISC-V target binaries
+  $(error PICOLIBC_LIBC not set. Point it at picolibc's libc.a)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy tests
#
# Two-target approach:
#   1. Build + run on x86 (host gcc + glibc) -> reference output
#   2. Build + run on RISC-V (cross gcc + picolibc via QEMU) -> target output
#   3. diff the outputs
#
# Usage:
#   make ref-bins       # Build x86 reference binaries
#   make target-bins    # Build RISC-V target binaries (needs CROSS_CC + PICOLIBC)
#   make clean

HOST_CC   ?= gcc
CROSS_CC  ?= riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS    ?= -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter

ifdef PICOLIBC
  SPECS = --specs=$(PICOLIBC)/picolibc.specs
else
  SPECS = --specs=picolibc.specs
endif

TESTS       = $(patsubst %.c,%,$(filter-out test-common.h,$(wildcard test-*.c)))
HEADERS     = test-common.h test-trig-data.h
REF_BINS    = $(addprefix ref/,$(TESTS))
TARGET_BINS = $(addprefix target/,$(TESTS))

.PHONY: ref-bins target-bins clean

ref-bins: $(REF_BINS)

target-bins: $(TARGET_BINS)

ref/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p ref
	$(HOST_CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

target/%: %.c $(HEADERS)
	@mkdir -p target
	$(CROSS_CC) $(SPECS) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $< -lm

clean:
	rm -rf ref target ref-out target-out

content.text:
The output is garbled — picolibc's stdio mixed with newlib's `libgloss_vh` I/O backend isn't clean. The `"..., 50) = 50` is semihosting trace leaking through.

The real issue: we need picolibc's math functions but a working I/O path. Let me extract just the math objects from picolibc's `libc.a` and make a standalone math-only archive that works cleanly with newlib.

input.command:
# List all math-related objects in picolibc's libc.a
/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-ar t /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/install-riscv64/picolibc/ndsv5-unknown-elf/lib/libc.a | grep -E '(^[ske]_|^[ew]f_|^sf_|math|rem_pio|fabs|sqrt|fma|fmax|fmin|copysign|lrint|lround|llrint|llround|fpclass|isinf|isnan|finite|pow|exp|log|sin|cos|atan)' | sort
input.description: Find math objects in picolibc's libc.a

content.text: Good — I can see the Andes hardfloat overrides in `libc.a` (e.g. `libm_machine_riscv_hardfloat_s_sin.c.o`). Let me extract just the `libm_*` objects into a standalone archive, then link that with newlib (which handles I/O cleanly with `-mvh`).

input.command:
cd /tmp
LIBC=/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/install-riscv64/picolibc/ndsv5-unknown-elf/lib/libc.a
AR=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-ar

# Extract all libm_* objects
mkdir -p libm-extract && cd libm-extract && rm -f *.o
$AR x "$LIBC" $($AR t "$LIBC" | grep '^libm_')
echo "Extracted $(ls *.o | wc -l) objects"

# Create standalone archive
$AR rcs /tmp/libm-picolibc.a *.o
echo "Created /tmp/libm-picolibc.a"
cd /tmp && rm -rf libm-extract

content.text:
The complete output from `/tmp/longprint` is:

```
AAAAAAAABBBBBBBBCCCCCCCCDDDDDDDD"..., 49) = 49
short
```

Confirmed — the qemu-runner agent truncates lines longer than ~32 characters. The `"..., 49) = 49` is semihosting write trace leaking through. This is a capture artifact, not a program bug.

The fix is simple: use shorter output lines. Let me shrink the format strings so each line fits within the capture buffer.

content.content:
1→/*
     2→ * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
     3→ *
     4→ * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
     5→ * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
     6→ * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
     7→ * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
     8→ *
     9→ * Output format: one line per test case
    10→ *   FUNCNAME INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX
    11→ */
    12→#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
    13→#define TEST_COMMON_H
    14→
    15→#include <stdio.h>
    16→#include <math.h>
    17→#include <stdint.h>
    18→#include <string.h>
    19→
    20→static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
    21→{
    22→    uint64_t u;
    23→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    24→    return u;
    25→}
    26→
    27→static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
    28→{
    29→    uint32_t u;
    30→    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    31→    return u;
    32→}
    33→
    34→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for double */
    35→#define P_D1(func, input) \
    36→    do { \
    37→        double _in = (input); \
    38→        double _r = func(_in); \
    39→        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
    40→               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    41→    } while (0)
    42→
    43→/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for float */
    44→#define P_F1(func, input) \

content.text: The double lines are ~50 chars (`%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n` = 12 + 1 + 16 + 4 + 16 + 1 = 50). I need to shorten them to under 32 chars. I'll drop the `%-12s` func name padding and use shorter format.

input.content:
/*
 * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
 *
 * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
 * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
 * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
 * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
 *
 * Output format: one line per test case.
 * Lines are kept short (<=30 chars) to avoid QEMU semihosting
 * capture truncation. Double hex is split across two puts() calls.
 */
#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
#define TEST_COMMON_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <string.h>

static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
{
    uint64_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
{
    uint32_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

/*
 * Output format for double (1-input):
 *   D funcname
 *   INPUT_HEX16 RESULT_HEX16
 *
 * Output format for float (1-input):
 *   F funcname INPUT8 RESULT8
 */

#define P_D1(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _r = func(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _r = func(_in); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
        uint64_t _au = d2u(_a), _bu = d2u(_b), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_au >> 32), (unsigned)_au, \
               (unsigned)(_bu >> 32), (unsigned)_bu); \
        printf(" %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b)); \
        printf(" %08x\n", (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        uint64_t _au = d2u(_a), _bu = d2u(_b); \
        uint64_t _cu = d2u(_c), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_au >> 32), (unsigned)_au, \
               (unsigned)(_bu >> 32), (unsigned)_bu); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_cu >> 32), (unsigned)_cu, \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b)); \
        printf(" %08x %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* func(input) -> int result */
#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %lld\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("F %s %08x %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
#define P_D1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("F %s %08x %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* fpclassify returns an int category */
#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D fpclassify\n"); \
        printf("%08x%08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("F fpclassify %08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* sincos: two outputs */
#define P_SINCOS(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _s, _c; \
        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        uint64_t _su = d2u(_s), _cu = d2u(_c); \
        printf("D sincos\n"); \
        printf("%08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_su >> 32), (unsigned)_su, \
               (unsigned)(_cu >> 32), (unsigned)_cu); \
    } while (0)

#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _s, _c; \
        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("F sincosf %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_in)); \
        printf(" %08x %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

/* --- Loop + special-value runners --- */

#define RUN_D1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_D1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_D1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_F1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_F1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_F1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOS_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOS((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOS(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOSF_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOSF((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOSF(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */

/*

 * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
 *
 * Output format: one line per test case.
 * Lines are kept short (<=30 chars) to avoid QEMU semihosting
 * capture truncation. Double hex is split across two puts() calls.
 */
#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
#define TEST_COMMON_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <string.h>

static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)

    uint64_t u;

}

static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)

}

/*
 * Output format for double (1-input):
 *   D funcname
 *   INPUT_HEX16 RESULT_HEX16
 *
 * Output format for float (1-input):
 *   F funcname INPUT8 RESULT8
 */

#define P_D1(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _r = func(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _r = func(_in); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
        uint64_t _au = d2u(_a), _bu = d2u(_b), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_au >> 32), (unsigned)_au, \
               (unsigned)(_bu >> 32), (unsigned)_bu); \
        printf(" %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b)); \
        printf(" %08x\n", (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        uint64_t _au = d2u(_a), _bu = d2u(_b); \
        uint64_t _cu = d2u(_c), _ru = d2u(_r); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_au >> 32), (unsigned)_au, \
               (unsigned)(_bu >> 32), (unsigned)_bu); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_cu >> 32), (unsigned)_cu, \
               (unsigned)(_ru >> 32), (unsigned)_ru); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("F %s %08x %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b)); \
        printf(" %08x %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* func(input) -> int result */
#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %lld\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("F %s %08x %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
#define P_D1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D %s\n", #func); \
        printf("%08x%08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("F %s %08x %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* fpclassify returns an int category */
#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        printf("D fpclassify\n"); \
        printf("%08x%08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu, _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("F fpclassify %08x %d\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* sincos: two outputs */
#define P_SINCOS(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _s, _c; \
        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        uint64_t _iu = d2u(_in); \
        uint64_t _su = d2u(_s), _cu = d2u(_c); \
        printf("D sincos\n"); \
        printf("%08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_iu >> 32), (unsigned)_iu); \
        printf("%08x%08x %08x%08x\n", \
               (unsigned)(_su >> 32), (unsigned)_su, \
               (unsigned)(_cu >> 32), (unsigned)_cu); \
    } while (0)

#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _s, _c; \
        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("F sincosf %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_in)); \
        printf(" %08x %08x\n", \
               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

/* --- Loop + special-value runners --- */

#define RUN_D1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_D1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_D1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_F1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_F1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_F1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOS_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOS((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOS(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOSF_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOSF((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOSF(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * test-common.h -- Result-printing framework for libm accuracy tests
 *
 * Design: Each test prints deterministic output lines.
 * Compile + run on x86 (glibc) to get reference output.
 * Compile + run on RISC-V (picolibc) to get target output.
 * diff the two. Any difference is a failure.
 *
 * Output format: one line per test case
 *   FUNCNAME INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX
 */
#ifndef TEST_COMMON_H
#define TEST_COMMON_H

#include <stdio.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <string.h>

static inline uint64_t d2u(double x)
{
    uint64_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

static inline uint32_t f2u(float x)
{
    uint32_t u;
    memcpy(&u, &x, sizeof(u));
    return u;
}

/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for double */
#define P_D1(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _r = func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Print: func INPUT_HEX -> RESULT_HEX for float */
#define P_F1(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _r = func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input double: func(a, b) */
#define P_D2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Two-input float: func(a, b) */
#define P_F2(func, a, b) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b); \
        printf("%-12s %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input double: func(a, b, c) — for fma */
#define P_D3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        double _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        double _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx %016llx %016llx -> %016llx\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_a), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_b), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* Three-input float: func(a, b, c) — for fmaf */
#define P_F3(func, a, b, c) \
    do { \
        float _a = (a), _b = (b), _c = (c); \
        float _r = func(_a, _b, _c); \
        printf("%-12s %08x %08x %08x -> %08x\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_a), (unsigned)f2u(_b), \
               (unsigned)f2u(_c), (unsigned)f2u(_r)); \
    } while (0)

/* func(input) -> int result */
#define P_D1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1I(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        long long _r = (long long)func(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %lld\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* Classification: func(input) -> bool (0 or 1) */
#define P_D1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_F1B(func, input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = !!(func(_in)); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
               #func, (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* fpclassify returns an int category */
#define P_FPCL_D(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %d\n", \
               "fpclassify", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

#define P_FPCL_F(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        int _r = fpclassify(_in); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %d\n", \
               "fpclassifyf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), _r); \
    } while (0)

/* sincos: two outputs */
#define P_SINCOS(input) \
    do { \
        double _in = (input); \
        double _s, _c; \
        sincos(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("%-12s %016llx -> %016llx %016llx\n", \
               "sincos", (unsigned long long)d2u(_in), \
               (unsigned long long)d2u(_s), (unsigned long long)d2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

#define P_SINCOSF(input) \
    do { \
        float _in = (input); \
        float _s, _c; \
        sincosf(_in, &_s, &_c); \
        printf("%-12s %08x -> %08x %08x\n", \
               "sincosf", (unsigned)f2u(_in), \
               (unsigned)f2u(_s), (unsigned)f2u(_c)); \
    } while (0)

/* --- Loop + special-value runners --- */

#define RUN_D1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_D1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_D1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_D1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_F1_SUITE(func, arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_F1(func, (arr)[_i]); \
        P_F1(func, INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, -INFINITY); \
        P_F1(func, NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOS_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOS((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOS(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOS(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#define RUN_SINCOSF_SUITE(arr, count) \
    do { \
        unsigned _i; \
        for (_i = 0; _i < (count); _i++) \
            P_SINCOSF((arr)[_i]); \
        P_SINCOSF(INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(-INFINITY); \
        P_SINCOSF(NAN); \
    } while (0)

#endif /* TEST_COMMON_H */
